content: Hallo. Vorbereitete Hilfeartikel in Ihrer Sprache finden Sie unter dem Link „Hilfeartikel“ oben auf dieser Seite. Freiwillige, die Ihre Sprache fließend beherrschen, beantworten Ihre Fragen jedoch auf einer anderen Website. Wenn Englisch also nicht Ihre bevorzugte Sprache ist, stellen Sie Ihre Fragen am besten auf der folgenden Website. ... Thunderbird-mail.de - http://www.thunderbird-mail.de/wiki/Hauptseite oder Hilfe - https://support.mozilla.org/de/kb/hilfe-durch-unsere-gemeinschaft-erhalten?exit_aaq=1
creator: siffemoz

content:
Another place where passwords or logon tokens can be cached is in a cookie. Try combing through Thunderbird's saved cookies and delete any that reference Hushmail.

They can be found (in Windows) at <Tools | Settings | Privacy & Security | Web Content | "Show Cookies" button>.

creator: sibedad

content: I checked with Northmail.ca, and they do restrict recipients to 25 [so much for Brave's AI], however they will review and possibly increase. Using mailing list does not work with Northmail,ca, as Thunderbird breaks them out, thus surpassing the threshold. Work around is to split mailing lists into groups of 25 or less.
creator: sibedad

content:
I suggest installing over the old one, assuming both versions came from thunderbird.net. To be totally safe (assuming Windows), you could do this to backup the profile:


  1. ensure thunderbird is not running
  2. enter %appdata% in search window on task bar and press Enter key
  3. this should open windows file explorer in Appdata\roaming folder
  4. highlight the Thunderbird folder and copy to external media
creator: davidsk

content:
Reinstalling Thunderbird went well, but, unfortunately did not solve the issues I've been having. Specifically, it keeps asking for my passwords, it no longer automatically downloads emails (I actually have to close the program and reopen it for that to happen) and it is moving emails into folders where they don't belong. I have a number of filters to direct emails to specific folders. The ones going into the wrong folders, however, have no filters associated with them and should just be in my inbox.

Should I mark this solved, as this particular question was about the issue of deleting and reinstalling Thunderbird and not about the issues I just mentioned?
creator: guavaman

content: YES, definitely. I always recommend focusing on the problem, and not on a possible solution. With a fresh post, you are more likely to get the attention of persons familiar with that issue. :)
creator: davidsk

content:
I tried tweaking the settings more and more by specifying the GPGME version:

mail.openpgp.load_untested_gpgme_version 45

This finally made it work. The error console wasn't displaying any useful diagnostics. This is clearly broken and needs to be fixed. I always had this problem when trying out the "new" Thunderbird versions, this time I persisted until solved. But it's supposed to just work. But I am really satisfied that I can finally use the new versions.
creator: dusk

content: Assuming you know the actual location of the 'extra' Local Folders, you can exit thunderbird and simplly copy the message file (ignore the msf entry) from one folder to the other. If not comfortable doing it that way, install the importeporttools NG addon, then highlight Local Folders, rightclick and select the importexport option to import mbox file, and then locate and select the desired message file to import. repeat for each desired message file.
creator: davidsk

content: Update: from my experience, the profile appears in different location only when you switch the source of the update. My updates have always been to the same place.

content: That is a feature in table view. At top of message view pane, just to the right of the quick filter is a small icon. Click that to change from card view to table view. You will then see the columns and you can select the FROM column and click to change sort order
